messages.content:
You want to set up a factory in Shenzhen to make a black and gold-coloured pen, and you want to call the pen a "MAN BLANK". Which of the following would you need to check?

		
If the design is patented.

		
If the combination of colours for such a pen is registered in any way as intellectual property.

		
If a near variant of your name for the pen is a trade mark.

		
All of the above.

		
None of the above
All of the above.

Which of the following you cannot do if “fair dealing” does not apply?

		
Seek permission from the copyright owner

		
Continue to use the copyright work

		
Use content from a source that is open access

		
Provide a link to the copyright work you want to use

		
Use other similar content available in the public domain
Continue to use the copyright work.

Which of the following can you use without obtaining the author’s permission?

		
Works outside of the copyright protection period.

		
Facts and short phrases.

		
Works in the public domain.

		
All of the above

		
None of the above
All of the above.

In Hong Kong, which of the following purposes can fair dealing be applied?

		
Research and private study.

		
Criticism, review and news reporting.

		
Giving or receiving instruction

		
Public administration.

		
All of the above.
All of the above.

Which of the following action(s) is/are regarded as plagiarism? 

		
Hiring someone to write your paper.

		
Building upon someone else's ideas without citation.

		
Copying a paragraph of text from a paper on the Web and inserting it in your own dissertation without acknowledgement.

		
Borrowing a paper from an alumnus who graduated in 1998 and submitting it as your own.

		
All of the above.
All of the above.

Which of the following is one of the permitted acts under “fair dealing” for research and private study under the Copyright Ordinance?

		
Copying of a non-substantial portion of the copyright work by a person other than the researcher himself or herself

		
Copying a non-substantial portion of the copyright work by the researcher himself / herself

		
Selling the copyright work for profit-making purposes

		
Making available the whole of the copyright work on the Internet for public use

		
Renting copies of the copyright work to the public
Copying a non-substantial portion of the copyright work by the researcher himself / herself.

Which of the following are the restricted acts provided to copyright owners under the Copyright Ordinance?

		
To perform, show or play the work in public

		
To issue copies of the work to the public

		
To make an adaptation of the work

		
To copy the work

		
All of the above
All of the above.

Which of the following works are protected by the current Copyright Ordinance in Hong Kong:

		
Books, computer software

		
Sound recordings made available on the Internet

		
Television programs provided by local television broadcasting compan

		
None of the above

		
All of the above
All of the above.

In determining whether any dealing with a work of any description is fair dealing, which of the following factor(s) has/have to be considered?

		
The purpose and nature of the dealing.

		
The nature of the work.

		
The amount and substantiality of the portion dealt with in relation to the work.

		
The effect of the dealing on the potential market for the work.

		
All of the above.
All of the above.

The Beatles issued their sound recording of their song Yellow Submarine in 1966. In what year does their intellectual property right to this recording expire?

		
2066

		
2016

		
2026

		
2000

		
1967

2036. The copyright protection period for sound recordings in Hong Kong is 50 years from the end of the calendar year in which the recording was first made, or if the recording was published during that period, until 50 years from the end of the calendar year in which it was first published. Since Yellow Submarine was recorded in 1966, the copyright protection period for this recording will expire on December 31, 2066.
The Beatles issued their sound recording of their song Yellow Submarine in 1966. In what year does their intellectual property right to this recording expire?

2036. The copyright protection period for sound recordings in Hong Kong is 50 years from the end of the calendar year in which the recording was first made, or if the recording was published during that period, until 50 years from the end of the calendar year in which it was first published. Since Yellow Submarine was recorded in 1966, the copyright protection period for this recording will expire on December 31, 2066.

Copyright arises automatically, without any registration, as soon as there is a record in some form of what has been created.

 True

 False
True.

Which of the followings is NOT an example of research data?

	A.	
Programming code

	B.	
Audio recording of an interview

	C.	
Survey

	D.	
Manuscript of a research paper

	E.	
Sequence of protein
D. Manuscript of a research paper.

Which of the following(s) is/are the benefits of having research data management? (Check all correct answers.)

		
Supporting open data

		
Increasing trust and transparency in research

		
Enhancing research impact

		
Saving time

		
Meeting funders’ and publishers’ requirements

- Supporting open data
- Increasing trust and transparency in research
- Enhancing research impact
- Meeting funders’ and publishers’ requirements

What do the FAIR data principles refer to? (Choose 4 answers)

		
Findability

		
Accountability

		
Accessibility

		
Interoperability

		
Responsibility

		
Reusability

- Findability
- Accessibility
- Interoperability
- Reusability

Where can you find the guidelines on Research Data Management?

	A.	
https://www.itsc.cuhk.edu.hk/all-it/

	B.	
https://www.orkts.cuhk.edu.hk/policy

	C.	
https://lib.cuhk.edu.hk/en/research/data

	D.	
https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/page/CodeofPracticeRPg
C. https://lib.cuhk.edu.hk/en/research/data

With which DMP online writing platform is CUHK affiliated?

		
DMPonline

		
Data Stewardship Wizard

		
DMPTool

		
easy.DMP
DMPonline.

Which of the followings is NOT included in the TRUST data repository principles?

		
Transparency

		
Reusability

		
User Focus

		
Sustainability

		
Technology
Technology.

Which of the following methods are used for anonymising sensitive data? (can choose multiple answers)

		
Masking

		
Aggregation

		
Hashing

- Masking
- Aggregation
- Hashing

Which of the followings is not a benefit of good data management practice?

		
Increasing the visibility of a research

		
Creating chances for collaborations

		
Opening all research data for public access

		
More efficient use of public resources
Opening all research data for public access.

Which of the following file names is not a good naming convention?

		
attendance 03 05 2022.txt

		
AlgorithmResults.csv

		
hypertension-symptoms.docx

		
plant_growth_stat.xlsx
attendance 03 05 2022.txt (spaces should not be used in file names).

Which of the followings are the elements of the FAIR data principles?

(1)    Findable
(2)    Accountable
(3)    Accessible
(4)    Interoperable
(5)    Responsible 
(6)    Reusable

		
(1), (2), (4), and (5) 

		
(1), (3), (4), and (6)

		
(2), (3), (5), and (6)

		
(1), (4), (5), and (6)

Which of the following statements about research data is incorrect?

		
Primary data should be used by its creators only.

		
Secondary data is data created by others for a purpose and now reused for another purpose.

		
Research data could be collected by observation, experiment, simulation, or compilation.

		
The sharing of research data could support the validation of research findings.
Primary data should be used by its creators only. (This is not true because primary data can be used by others with the proper permissions or data sharing agreements.)

What are the advantages of online DMP writing platform?

(1)    Availability of DMP templates from popular funding agencies
(2)    Availability of DMP rubrics
(3)    Availability of guidelines of different funding agencies and academic institutions

		
(1) and (2)

		
(2) and (3)

		
(1) and (3)

		
(1), (2), and (3)

Which of the followings is not a reason for writing DMPs?

		
To identify the weaknesses of a project in early stage

		
To promote TRUST data repository principles

		
To meet the requirement of funding agencies

		
To plan for human resources and financial support
To promote TRUST data repository principles (While TRUST is an important aspect of data management, it is not a reason for writing DMPs.)

Which of the following types of metadata should be accompanied with the data deposited in a data repository to support discovery of data?

		
Citation Metadata

		
Geospatial Metadata

		
Humanities Metadata

		
Social Science Metadata
Citation Metadata.

Which of the following statements about data repository is incorrect?

		
A trustworthy data repository should provide each dataset a unique and permanent identifier (e.g. digital object identifier DOI).

		
A trustworthy data repository upgrades its software and hardware standards according to the development of technology.

		
A general data repository accommodates the data needs of a specific research community. 

		
A data repository is a storage space for researchers to curate and share data for reuse.

A general data repository accommodates the data needs of a specific research community. (This is contradictory. A general data repository is designed to accommodate the needs of a wide range of research communities rather than a specific community.)

Which of the followings are direct identifiers?

(1)    Telephone number
(2)    IP address
(3)    Ethnicity
(4)    Photographs
(5)    Medical record number
(6)    Gender

		
(1), (2), (4), and (5)

		
(1), (3), (5), and (6)

		
(2), (3), (4), and (6)

		
(1), (4), (5), and (6)

Which of the followings are direct identifiers?

(1) Telephone number
(2) IP address
(3) Ethnicity
(4) Photographs
(5) Medical record number
(6) Gender

Which of the followings is a proper method to handle sensitive and confidential data?

		
Data should be anonymised before and during the research process.

		
Data should be anonymised during and after the research process.

		
Data should be anonymised after the research process.

		
Data should be anonymised only when it is published in a data repository.
Data should be anonymised before and during the research process.
